content: Three Discord libs draw the framework-vs-app line differently. discord.py ext.commands uses Cogs (classes grouping commands/listeners) loaded as 'extensions' via explicit bot.add_cog()/load_extension(); modern reusable persistent components use discord.ui.View(timeout=None)+stable custom_id registered with add_view() in setup_hook, and discord.ui.DynamicItem (2.4.0+) encodes per-component state in a custom_id regex template (<=100 chars) to avoid a database. hikari is deliberately minimal: only event listeners (.set_listener), no built-in command/prefix system — users pair it with a separate command framework (Tanjun) for the app layer. interactions.py renames Cogs to 'Extensions' that self-register (MyExt(bot), no add_cog). The pattern: the LIBRARY owns transport+component plumbing+registration mechanism; the APP owns the command set and content. Self-registration vs explicit registration is the main divergence.
